combinedCode:
# ── BFS Maze — drag to build walls, click to explore ─────────────────────────
# Interactive mode only — there is no algorithm to trace.
# After clicking Analyze, click "Finish & Interact" to start.
#
#  • Drag any wall block:
#      → onto an empty board cell  → it stays there
#      → onto an occupied cell     → it snaps back to where it started
#      → outside the board         → it disappears
#  • Drag the orange source block (left of board):
#      → a new wall block is spawned and follows your mouse
#      → drop it on an empty board cell to place it
#  • Click any empty board cell → BFS fills reachable cells (shows distance)
#  • Click the Clear button → reset fill, click again for a new run
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import random
from time import time
from collections import deque

# ── Board constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BOARD_W, BOARD_H = 10, 10       # width and height in cells

# ── Colors ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EMPTY_COLOR  = (220, 225, 235)
WALL_COLOR   = (45,  50,  65)
FILL_COLOR   = (150, 180, 255)
START_COLOR  = (80,  200, 120)
SOURCE_COLOR = (220, 110,  60)
BTN_COLOR    = (80,  100, 130)

# ── Global state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BLOCK = -2
EMPTY = -1
board = [[-1] * BOARD_W for _ in range(BOARD_H)]
clear_board = True

board[1][1] = BLOCK
board[2][1] = BLOCK
board[3][1] = BLOCK
board[0][8] = BLOCK
board[1][8] = BLOCK

for j in range(1,8):
    board[2][j] = BLOCK

def empty_cells():
    global board, clear_board
    board = [
        [BLOCK if board[i][j] == BLOCK else EMPTY 
        for j in range(BOARD_W)]
        for i in range(BOARD_H)
    ]
    clear_board = True
    # @viz
    update_viz_board()
    # @end

def in_board(row, col):
    return (0 <= row < BOARD_H and 0 <= col < BOARD_W)

# ── BFS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def do_bfs(start_r, start_c):
    global clear_board
    if not clear_board:
        return
    clear_board = False
    start_time = time()
    if not in_board(start_r, start_c):
        print(f'Must start in a valid cell inside {BOARD_H} x {BOARD_W}, \n'+
              f'instead got ({start_r},{start_c})')
        return
    if board[start_r][start_c] == BLOCK:
        print(f'Cannot start at a block ({start_r},{start_c})')

    board[start_r][start_c] = 0
    # @viz
    update_viz_block(start_r, start_c)
    # @end
    queue   = deque([(start_r, start_c, 0)])
    visited = {(start_r, start_c)}
    while queue:
        print(time()-start_time)
        r, c, dist = queue.popleft()
        for dr, dc in [(-1, 0), (1, 0), (0, -1), (0, 1)]:
            nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
            if (in_board(nr, nc) and board[nr][nc] == EMPTY):
                visited.add((nr, nc))
                board[nr][nc] = dist + 1
                # @viz
                update_viz_block(nr, nc)
                # @end
                queue.append((nr, nc, dist + 1))

# ── Element classes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# @viz

rect = Rect(width=BOARD_W, height=BOARD_H, alpha=0, z=31)
rect.on_click = lambda x, y,: do_bfs(y, x)

panel = Panel(x=1, y=1)
arr = Array2D(cells=board, show_index=False, z=-1)
panel.add(arr, rect)

viz_board = [
    [Rect(x=j, y=i)
    for j in range(BOARD_W)]
    for i in range(BOARD_H)
]

for row in range(BOARD_H):
    for col in range(BOARD_W):
        panel.add(viz_board[row][col])

def update_viz_block(row: int, col: int):
    viz_cell = viz_board[row][col]
    cell = board[row][col]
    viz_cell.alpha = 1*(cell<0)
    viz_cell.color = WALL_COLOR if (cell==BLOCK) else EMPTY_COLOR

    arr.cells = board

def update_viz_board():
    for row in range(BOARD_H):
        for col in range(BOARD_W):
            update_viz_block(row, col)

update_viz_board()

# ── Clear button (below board) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Label created before ClearBtn so ClearBtn wins the objects-map entry
clear_btn = Rect(width=2, color=(100,180,130), y=BOARD_H+1, x=BOARD_W//2-1)
clear_btn.on_click = lambda x, y: empty_cells()

panel.add(
    Label(label='clear', width=2, y=BOARD_H+1, x=BOARD_W//2-1),
    clear_btn
)

# @end
